title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: Phylogenomic studies routinely produce multiple trees different methods datasets. researchers analyse set taxa using IQ-TREE, GHOST, ASTRAL — using different data types Ultraconserved Elements (UCEs) partitioned sequence data — often obtain slightly different topologies different levels node support. disagreement analyses called incongruence (Steenwyk et al. 2023). problem incongruence exists. problem communicating honestly. Current practice publish one tree — usually maximum likelihood tree — move everything else supplementary materials. reader looking published figure tell nodes robust across five analyses nodes contested. transparency problem.

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: rug plot method addresses drawing compact colour-coded grid internal node backbone tree. cell grid represents one alternative analysis, shaded white (clade absent support) black (full support). Nodes supported unanimously analyses shown solid dot — maximally clean. Nodes analyses disagree show mixed grid reader can interpret glance. method first introduced Wheeler (1995) name space plots, later called Navajo rugs (Giribet 2003) topological congruence plots (Wheeler & Hayashi 1998). Despite thirty years use literature, automated R tool existed produce visualizations. phylorugD1 first implementation.

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: researcher : runs one phylogenomic analysis dataset uses IQ-TREE, ASTRAL, GHOST, RAxML, MrBayes, BEAST wants show node support multiple analyses single figure works R needs reproducible, scriptable workflow package organism-agnostic. developed demonstrated dung beetles works phylogenomic dataset.

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("mdrifathahamed/phylorugD1")

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: vignette uses phylogenomic dataset 289 dung beetle taxa (Coleoptera: Scarabaeinae Aphodiinae) assembled Tarasov Lab Finnish Museum Natural History (LUOMUS), University Helsinki. Five analyses performed: IQ-TREE UCE tree used backbone — data-rich, methodologically standard analysis. four comparison trees.

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: read_trees_from_dir() reads tree files folder named list phylo objects. supports Newick NEXUS formats.
code: library(phylorugD1) library(readxl) library(phytools)  raw_trees <- read_trees_from_dir(   dir    = "data/raw_trees/70p",   ext    = "tre",   format = "newick" )  names(raw_trees) # [1] "70p_ASTRAL_partition_entropy.tre" "70p_ASTRAL_uce.tre" # [3] "70p_ghost.tre"                    "70p_partition_entropy.tre" # [5] "70p_uce.tre"

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: outgroup species must removed translation tip labels still raw specimen code format stage.
code: rooted_trees <- lapply(raw_trees, function(tr) {   root(tr,        outgroup     = c("NicorbUCE", "NicvesUCE"),        resolve.root = TRUE) })

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: translate_tree_tips() renames tip labels museum specimen codes full species names using lookup table stored biogeo.xlsx. lookup table two columns: (original code) (species name).
code: biogeo <- read_excel("data/biogeo.xlsx", sheet = "BioGeo")  translated_trees <- lapply(rooted_trees, function(tr) {   translate_tree_tips(     phy      = tr,     data     = biogeo,     from_col = "from",     to_col   = "to"   ) })  head(translated_trees[[1]]$tip.label) # [1] "Amphistomus_primonactus_Amppri_UCE" # [2] "Coptodactyla_brooksi_CopbroUCE"

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: outgroup removed using phytools::findMRCA() locate recent common ancestor ingroup, extract.clade() keep ingroup taxa. reduces dataset 316 289 taxa.
code: processed_trees <- lapply(translated_trees, function(tr) {   tr   <- ladderize(tr)   node <- phytools::findMRCA(     tr,     c("Heteronitis_ragazzii_STL10032",       "Aphodius_immundus_ST003")   )   extract.clade(tr, node) }) names(processed_trees) <- names(raw_trees)  Ntip(processed_trees[[1]]) # [1] 289

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: check_same_taxa() confirms five trees share exactly 289 taxa downstream analysis. must return TRUE continuing.
code: check_same_taxa(processed_trees) # ✅ All trees contain the same set of taxa. # [1] TRUE

what: Step 6 — Define backbone and comparison trees
title: Getting started with phylorugD1
code: backbone  <- processed_trees$`70p_uce.tre`  tree_list <- processed_trees[c(   "70p_partition_entropy.tre",   "70p_ghost.tre",   "70p_ASTRAL_uce.tre",   "70p_ASTRAL_partition_entropy.tre" )]

what: Step 7 — Build the node presence matrix
title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: node_presence_matrix() core function. 288 internal nodes backbone tree, searches comparison tree clade records whether present. use_support = FALSE, returns binary matrix 1 means clade exists analysis 0 means . Node 292 real biological result. clade node exists IQ-TREE partitioned, ASTRAL analyses, GHOST. topological incongruence — genuine disagreement methods whether species form group.
code: rug_mt <- node_presence_matrix(   backbone    = backbone,   trees       = tree_list,   use_support = FALSE )  dim(rug_mt) # [1] 288   5  head(rug_mt) #      node_id  70p_partition  70p_ghost  70p_ASTRAL_uce  70p_ASTRAL_part # [1,]     290            1          1               1               1 # [2,]     291            1          1               1               1 # [3,]     292            1          0               1               1

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: rug_layout_map() assigns analysis fixed position 2×2 grid. layout every node across entire tree, reader can learn interpret whole figure. grid positions :

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: rf_similarity_plot() computes Robinson-Foulds distance every pair trees projects result 2D using Multidimensional Scaling (MDS). Trees close together plot similar topologies. Trees far apart disagree substantially.
code: all_trees        <- processed_trees names(all_trees) <- c("ASTRAL_part", "ASTRAL_uce",                       "GHOST", "IQTREE_part", "IQTREE_uce") class(all_trees) <- "multiPhylo"  rf_similarity_plot(all_trees)

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: plot_node_rug() must called plot.phylo() — reads node coordinates active plot environment draws rug grid uncertain node.
code: # Colour palette: white = absent, black = full support pal_info        <- list() pal_info$pal    <- gray.colors(64, start = 0.95, end = 0.00) pal_info$breaks <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 64)  map_to_color <- function(val, pal_info) {   if (val == 0) return("white")   pal_info$pal[cut(val, breaks = pal_info$breaks,                    include.lowest = TRUE)] }  # Split nodes rug_mt_variable <- rug_mt[   !apply(rug_mt[, -1, drop = FALSE], 1,          function(x) all(x == 1)), , drop = FALSE]  rug_mt_unanimous <- rug_mt[   apply(rug_mt[, -1, drop = FALSE], 1,         function(x) all(x == 1)), , drop = FALSE]  # Plot backbone tree plot.phylo(backbone, show.tip.label = TRUE, cex = 0.4,            label.offset = 0.001, no.margin = TRUE, edge.width = 1.5)  # Solid dots at unanimous nodes last_pp  <- get("last_plot.phylo", envir = ape::.PlotPhyloEnv) node_ids <- rug_mt_unanimous[, 1] points(last_pp$xx[node_ids], last_pp$yy[node_ids],        pch = 16, cex = 0.6, col = "black")  # Compute cell size dy         <- median(diff(sort(last_pp$yy[1:Ntip(backbone)]))) x_per_inch <- diff(last_pp$x.lim) / par("pin")[1] y_per_inch <- diff(last_pp$y.lim) / par("pin")[2] cell_h     <- dy * 0.18 cell_w     <- cell_h * (x_per_inch / y_per_inch)  # Draw rug grid plot_node_rug(   tree         = backbone,   rug_mt       = rug_mt_variable,   cell_h       = cell_h,   cell_w       = cell_w,   x_offset     = 0.04,   y_offset     = 0,   map_to_color = map_to_color,   pal_info     = pal_info )

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: Solid black dot — four comparison analyses support node maximum support. node robust. investigation needed. Coloured grid — least one analysis differs. Read grid: Black cell — clade present fully supported analysis White cell — clade absent analysis entirely Grey cell — clade present partial support (use_support = TRUE) node IQ-TREE cells black ASTRAL cells white means maximum likelihood coalescent methods disagree. biologically meaningful — may indicate incomplete lineage sorting rapid diversification node.

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: Two parameters control visual appearance: large trees (200+ taxa) recommend saving PDF width = 16 height = 40 give node enough space.
code: cell_h <- dy * 0.18   # increase → bigger cells                       # decrease → smaller cells  x_offset <- 0.04     # increase → grid moves further right of node                      # decrease → grid sits closer to node

title: Getting started with phylorugD1
text: Giribet, G. (2003). Stability phylogenetic formulations relationship nodal support. Systematic Biology, 52(4), 554–564. Steenwyk, J. L., Li, Y., Zhou, X., Shen, X. X., & Rokas, . (2023). Incongruence phylogenomics era. Nature Reviews Genetics, 24(12), 834–850. Wheeler, W. C. (1995). Sequence alignment, parameter sensitivity, phylogenetic analysis molecular data. Systematic Biology, 44(3), 321–331. Wheeler, W. C., & Hayashi, C. Y. (1998). phylogeny extant chelicerate orders. Cladistics, 14, 173–192.

what: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
text: Phylogenomic studies routinely produce multiple trees different methods datasets — yet dedicated R tool existed visualize node support across simultaneously single reference topology. phylorugD1 fills gap implementing rug plot method: compact colour-coded grid drawn internal node backbone tree, cell represents one analysis shaded white (absent support) black (full support). researcher working IQ-TREE, GHOST, ASTRAL, MrBayes, combination phylogenomic methods can use phylorugD1 communicate results honestly completely.

title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("mdrifathahamed/phylorugD1")

title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
code: library(phylorugD1) library(readxl) library(phytools)  # Step 1 — Read raw trees raw_trees <- read_trees_from_dir("data/raw_trees/70p", ext = "tre")  # Step 2 — Root before translating (use raw tip labels for outgroup) rooted_trees <- lapply(raw_trees, function(tr) {   root(tr, outgroup = c("NicorbUCE", "NicvesUCE"), resolve.root = TRUE) })  # Step 3 — Translate specimen codes to species names biogeo <- read_excel("data/biogeo.xlsx", sheet = "BioGeo") translated_trees <- lapply(rooted_trees, function(tr) {   translate_tree_tips(tr, data = biogeo, from_col = "from", to_col = "to") })  # Step 4 — Extract ingroup processed_trees <- lapply(translated_trees, function(tr) {   tr   <- ladderize(tr)   node <- phytools::findMRCA(     tr,     c("Heteronitis_ragazzii_STL10032", "Aphodius_immundus_ST003")   )   extract.clade(tr, node) }) names(processed_trees) <- names(raw_trees)  # Step 5 — Validate taxa check_same_taxa(processed_trees)  # Step 6 — Define backbone and comparison trees backbone  <- processed_trees$`70p_uce.tre` tree_list <- processed_trees[c(   "70p_partition_entropy.tre",   "70p_ghost.tre",   "70p_ASTRAL_uce.tre",   "70p_ASTRAL_partition_entropy.tre" )]  # Step 7 — Build node support matrix rug_mt <- node_presence_matrix(   backbone    = backbone,   trees       = tree_list,   use_support = FALSE )  # Step 8 — Build grid layout layout_df <- rug_layout_map(rug_mt, n_rows = 2, n_cols = 2)  # Step 9 — RF similarity plot all_trees        <- processed_trees class(all_trees) <- "multiPhylo" rf_similarity_plot(all_trees)  # Step 10 — Draw the rug plot pal_info        <- list() pal_info$pal    <- gray.colors(64, start = 0.95, end = 0.00) pal_info$breaks <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 64)  map_to_color <- function(val, pal_info) {   if (val == 0) return("white")   pal_info$pal[cut(val, breaks = pal_info$breaks, include.lowest = TRUE)] }  rug_mt_variable <- rug_mt[   !apply(rug_mt[, -1, drop = FALSE], 1, function(x) all(x == 1)),   , drop = FALSE ]  plot.phylo(backbone, show.tip.label = TRUE, cex = 0.4,            label.offset = 0.001, no.margin = TRUE, edge.width = 1.5)  last_pp    <- get("last_plot.phylo", envir = ape::.PlotPhyloEnv) dy         <- median(diff(sort(last_pp$yy[1:Ntip(backbone)]))) x_per_inch <- diff(last_pp$x.lim) / par("pin")[1] y_per_inch <- diff(last_pp$y.lim) / par("pin")[2] cell_h     <- dy * 0.18 cell_w     <- cell_h * (x_per_inch / y_per_inch)  plot_node_rug(   tree         = backbone,   rug_mt       = rug_mt_variable,   cell_h       = cell_h,   cell_w       = cell_w,   x_offset     = 0.04,   y_offset     = 0,   map_to_color = map_to_color,   pal_info     = pal_info )

title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
text: package demonstrated phylogenomic dataset 289 dung beetle taxa (Coleoptera: Scarabaeinae Aphodiinae) analysed across five methods — IQ-TREE partitioned, IQ-TREE UCE, GHOST partitioned, ASTRAL UCE, ASTRAL partitioned — assembled Tarasov Lab Finnish Museum Natural History (LUOMUS), University Helsinki.

title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
text: Ahamed, M. R. & Tarasov, S. (2026). phylorugD1: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses Single Reference Tree. R package version 0.1.0. https://github.com/mdrifathahamed/phylorugD1

title: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses
text: Prototype R script Dr. Sergei Tarasov, Tarasov Lab, LUOMUS, University Helsinki. MSc project supervised Dr. Sergei Tarasov.

what: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
title: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
text: Compares list phylogenetic trees reference tree (first tree list) ensure trees contain exact set taxa (tip labels). discrepancies found, detailed diagnostic reports listing missing extra taxa provided.

title: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
code: check_same_taxa(tree_list, verbose = TRUE)

title: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
text: tree_list list phylogenetic tree objects class "phylo", multiPhylo object containing trees compared. verbose Logical. TRUE (default), detailed status messages diagnostic reports mismatched trees printed console using message.

title: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
text: single logical value. TRUE trees contain identical taxa. FALSE tree differs, case pipeline proceed.

title: Check Taxa Consistency Across Multiple Phylogenetic Trees — check_same_taxa
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming 'my_trees' is a named list of phylo objects loaded into R: trees <- read_trees_from_dir("path/to/your/trees") result <- check_same_taxa(trees)  # Check if it's safe to proceed: if (result) {   message("Safe to proceed.") } } # }

what: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
title: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
text: Evaluates collection alternative phylogenetic trees designated reference backbone topology. internal node backbone tree, function determines whether matching monophyletic clade exists comparison tree, populating coordinate data framework matrix binary occupancy flags quantitative branch support scores.

title: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
code: node_presence_matrix(   backbone,   trees,   use_support = FALSE,   support_col = 1,   round_support = NULL )

title: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
text: backbone phylogenetic tree object class "phylo". tree serves structural reference template, internal nodes establishes mapping row coordinate final output matrix. trees named list alternative phylogenetic tree objects class "phylo", object class "multiPhylo". tree list corresponds single independent data partition, inference method, bootstrap replicate, defining unique data mapping column output matrix. use_support Logical. FALSE (default), function operates binary presence/absence mode, returning 1 backbone clade congruent comparison tree 0 otherwise. TRUE, function operates quantitative mode, returning literal branch support score extracted matching clades, 0 clade completely absent structurally discordant. support_col Integer. Specifies target column data extract internal node indexing tables (typically column 1 2, mapping onto distinct bootstrap posterior probability metrics). Assessed use_support = TRUE. Defaults 1. round_support Integer NULL. integer supplied, numerical support values rounded specified number decimal places matrix insertion. Defaults NULL.

title: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
text: numeric matrix : Rows: Represent internal nodes backbone tree, absolute backbone node index stored first column labeled "node_id". Columns: Represent target tree supplied inside tree_list. Column names automatically mapped onto names list elements, padded sequentially "tree_1", "tree_2" names absent. Cells: Contain numeric scalar values indicating binary clade presence (0 1) quantitative statistical confidence values (e.g., bootstrap percentages Bayesian posterior values) depending use_support.

title: Construct Node Presence and Support Value Matrix Across Multiple Phylogenies — node_presence_matrix
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Generate a shared backbone tree and several alternative trees shared_backbone <- ape::rtree(5, tip.label = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E")) alternative_trees <- list(   UCE_partition = ape::rtree(5, tip.label = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E")),   Mito_partition = ape::rtree(5, tip.label = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E")) )  # 1. Calculate binary presence/absence matrix topo_matrix <- node_presence_matrix(   backbone    = shared_backbone,   trees   = alternative_trees,   use_support = FALSE )  # 2. Calculate continuous support value intensity matrix support_matrix <- node_presence_matrix(   backbone    = shared_backbone,   trees   = alternative_trees,   use_support = TRUE,   support_col = 1 ) } # }

what: phylorugD1: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses — phylorugD1-package
title: phylorugD1: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses — phylorugD1-package
text: Provides functions visualise node support values multiple independent phylogenomic analyses simultaneously single reference tree using rug plot method. Although researchers routinely run multiple phylogenomic analyses, publish one tree hide rest supplementary materials, yet dedicated R tool exists visualise node support across multiple analyses, leaving researchers unable communicate phylogenomic uncertainty transparently single publication figure.

title: phylorugD1: Visualize Node Support Across Multiple Phylogenomic Analyses — phylorugD1-package
text: Maintainer: Md Rifath Ahamed rifath.ahamed@helsinki.fi (ORCID) Authors: Md Rifath Ahamed rifath.ahamed@helsinki.fi (ORCID) Sergei Tarasov sergei.tarasov@helsinki.fi (ORCID)

what: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
title: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
text: Superimposes compact color-coded grid sub-cells ("rug plots") onto internal branching nodes previously plotted phylogenetic tree. provides clear, multi-analysis visual representation statistical node support values.

title: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
code: plot_node_rug(   tree,   rug_mt,   cell_h,   cell_w,   x_offset = 0.02,   y_offset = 0,   map_to_color,   pal_info,   n_cols = 3 )

title: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
text: tree object class "phylo" representing reference topology rendered active graphics device. rug_mt matrix data frame first column corresponds backbone node_id sequence, subsequent columns contain numeric support values comparison tree. cell_h numeric value specifying geometric height individual grid sub-cell plotting coordinate space. cell_w numeric value specifying geometric width individual grid sub-cell plotting coordinate space. x_offset numeric scalar multiplier used shift grid horizontally away node vertex. Expressed relative tree's maximum horizontal width. Default 0.02. y_offset numeric scalar multiplier used shift grid vertically away node vertex. Expressed relative tree's maximum vertical height. Default 0. map_to_color function accepts numeric support value alongside palette details object outputs valid character color string. pal_info configuration object (list) storing custom color keys, value breaks, mapping criteria requested map_to_color. n_cols integer specifying number horizontal columns inside individual rug plot grid layout. Default 3.

title: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
text: Invoked exclusively side-effect rendering geometric shapes onto active plot window. Returns NULL invisibly.

title: Plot Node Rug Grids Over a Phylogenetic Canvas — plot_node_rug
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  set.seed(42) my_tree <- ape::rtree(6) ape::plot.phylo(my_tree)  # Mock matrix for 5 internal nodes and 3 comparison trees support_mat <- data.frame(   node_id = 7:11,   Analysis1 = c(1, 0.9, 1, 0.5, 0.8),   Analysis2 = c(0.95, 0.85, 1, 0.3, 0.9),   Analysis3 = c(1, 0.8, 0.9, 0.4, 1) )  # Simple dummy mapping function custom_mapper <- function(val, pal) {   if (val >= 0.9) {     return("darkgreen")   }   if (val >= 0.7) {     return("chartreuse3")   }   return("firebrick1") }  plot_node_rug(   tree = my_tree, rug_mt = support_mat,   cell_h = 0.2, cell_w = 0.05,   x_offset = 0.03, y_offset = 0.1,   map_to_color = custom_mapper, pal_info = list(),   n_cols = 3 ) } # }

what: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
title: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
text: Searches specified directory phylogenetic tree files matching file extension imports R list tree objects. supports Newick NEXUS formats wrapping corresponding ape package functions. resulting list elements automatically named using base names respective files easy tracking.

title: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
code: read_trees_from_dir(dir, ext = "tre", format = c("newick", "nexus"))

title: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
text: dir Character string. path directory containing tree files. ext Character string. file extension search (without leading dot). Defaults "tre". format Character string. file format trees, either "newick" "nexus". Defaults "newick".

title: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
text: named list objects class "phylo". element one tree. List names filenames trees read .

title: Reads Multiple Phylogenetic Trees from a Directory — read_trees_from_dir
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ trees <- read_trees_from_dir(   dir    = "path/to/your/trees",   ext    = "tre",   format = "newick" ) } # }

what: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
title: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
text: Calculates symmetric topological Robinson-Foulds (RF) distance pairs input trees, projects structural proximity patterns onto lower-dimensional continuous plane using Classical Multidimensional Scaling (MDS), renders labeled ordination scatter plot current graphics device.

title: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
code: rf_similarity_plot(trees, method = "PH85", k = 2)

title: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
text: trees object class "multiPhylo" containing list independent phylogenetic trees sharing identical terminal leaf-set. method Character string. distance method passed ape::dist.topo(). Use "PH85" Robinson-Foulds symmetric difference (default). k Integer. Number dimensions MDS projection. Default 2 standard 2D scatter plot.

title: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
text: MDS coordinate matrix invisibly. scatter plot drawn current graphics device side effect.

title: Map and Plot Multidimensional Tree Similarity from Robinson-Foulds Distances — rf_similarity_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Generate a list of 5 mock random trees with identical tip labels set.seed(42) mock_trees <- replicate(5, ape::rtree(10, tip.label = paste0("Taxon_", 1:10)),   simplify = FALSE ) class(mock_trees) <- "multiPhylo" names(mock_trees) <- paste0("Analysis_", 1:5)  # Create the plots/ directory if missing and run the visualization pipeline rf_similarity_plot(mock_trees) } # }

what: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
title: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
text: Transforms wide node-support matrix long-format data frame containing specific row, column, sequential index positions comparison tree within compact color-coded rug plot grid. function essential mapping multiple tree analyses individual sub-cells backbone node.

title: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
code: rug_layout_map(rug_mt, n_rows = 2, n_cols = 3)

title: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
text: rug_mt matrix data frame first column contains reference node_id values (numeric character), subsequent columns contain names labels phylogenetic comparison trees. n_rows integer specifying maximum number grid rows arrange tree cells vertically. Default 2. n_cols integer specifying maximum number grid columns arrange tree cells horizontally. Default 3.

title: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
text: long-format data frame one row per node per analysis five columns: node_id Internal node identifier backbone tree. tree_name Name comparison tree. row Row position rug grid (1 = top). col Column position rug grid (1 = left). cell_index Sequential index analysis.

title: Map Phylogenetic Trees to Grid Cell Coordinates for Rug Plots — rug_layout_map
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example support matrix for 3 reference nodes across 5 alternative tree analyses mock_matrix <- matrix(   c(     10, 1, 0.95, 0.80, 1, 0,     11, 0, 1.00, 1.00, 0, 1,     12, 1, 0.50, 0.00, 1, 1   ),   nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE ) colnames(mock_matrix) <- c("node_id", "TreeA", "TreeB", "TreeC", "TreeD", "TreeE")  # Map the positions onto a 2x3 grid layout grid_layout <- rug_layout_map(mock_matrix, n_rows = 2, n_cols = 3) head(grid_layout) } # }

what: Translate Tip Labels of a Phylogenetic Tree — translate_tree_tips
title: Translate Tip Labels of a Phylogenetic Tree — translate_tree_tips
text: Renames terminal tip labels phylogenetic tree using lookup table supplied data frame. tips matching entries from_col renamed — unmatched tips left unchanged. from_col to_col must character columns factors, summary message reports many tips translated.

title: Translate Tip Labels of a Phylogenetic Tree — translate_tree_tips
code: translate_tree_tips(phy, data, from_col = "from", to_col = "to")

title: Translate Tip Labels of a Phylogenetic Tree — translate_tree_tips
text: phy phylogenetic tree object class "phylo". data data frame containing label translation lookup table. Can standard data.frame tibble. from_col character string specifying column name data holds current tip labels tree. Defaults "". to_col character string specifying column name data holds new desired replacement labels. Defaults "".
